tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web for current information. Returns titles, URLs, and text snippets for the top results. Use when the user asks about recent events, wants to look something up online, or needs information you don't already know.

query.description: The search query (e.g. 'SpaceX Starship launch 2026', 'best TypeScript ORM')

count.description: Number of results to return (1-10, default: 5)

market.description: Country code for localized results (e.g. 'us', 'nl'). Default: 'us'

useWhen: the user needs fresh public-web information that is not already in the local skill set
tools.returnsSummaryShape: top web search results with titles, urls, and snippets

tools.name: web_fetch_page
tools.description: Fetch and read the text content of any public web page or URL. Returns extracted readable text from HTML pages, up to 8 KB. Use when the user asks you to read, browse, visit, open, or summarize a specific URL or web page.

url.description: The full URL to fetch (must use http:// or https://)

useWhen: the user wants to read or summarize a specific web page or URL
tools.returnsSummaryShape: final URL, content-type header, and extracted readable text (up to 8 KB)

shortDescription: Search the web (Brave Search API or DuckDuckGo fallback) and fetch/read the text content of specific URLs
orchestratorUseCases: look up recent public information on the open web
read or summarize a specific webpage or URL

externalAccountsNeeded.description: Brave Search API key (optional — skill falls back to DuckDuckGo Instant Answer when absent)
externalAccountsNeeded.howToObtain: Free tier at https://api.search.brave.com/; store key in stamp Key Vault as BraveSearchApiKey for full-quality results

maintenanceTasks.name: verify-brave-api-key
maintenanceTasks.description: Verify Brave Search API key is valid by making a test query
